ops.title: cc-interact: ReadHeaderTimeout on the shared http.Server (slowloris)
ops.description: The daemon's shared http.Server (daemon/server.go:317-321, v0.12.0) sets only Handler+BaseContext — no ReadHeaderTimeout/ReadTimeout/IdleTimeout. Any peer that sends one byte then stalls parks a serving goroutine indefinitely (plain: readRequest; TLS via cc-present's sniffed tailnet legs: the handshake). Pre-existing exposure class on every listener including loopback — surfaced by cc-present's tailnet-TLS review (fable refuter, 2026-07-20): cc-present's sniffer closes zero-byte conns after 3s but cannot bound anything past the first byte. Fix belongs here: ReadHeaderTimeout (and consider IdleTimeout) on the shared server; SSE streams need WriteTimeout left unset. Consumers pick it up on the next cc-interact release.

ops.text: Shared http.Server sets ReadHeaderTimeout (SSE-safe: no WriteTimeout)
A one-byte-then-stall conn is reaped instead of parking a goroutine indefinitely (test)
cc-present bumps to the cc-interact release carrying it
